title: SS19/15 – Exercising certain functions under the Building Societies Act 1986 2.12

snippet: The exemptions in section 9A of the 1986 Act fall into two broad categories: (a) those which allow a society or subsidiary undertaking to provide certain retail services to its customers, including: (i) acting as market maker in currency or securities transactions of less than £100,000; (ii) trading in currencies (but not commodities) up to a value of £100,000 per transaction; (iii) entering into contracts for differences in respect of customers who wish to hedge exposures arising from their own loans or deposits with the society or a connected undertaking; or (iv) acting as market maker or entering into derivative investments in its capacity as manager of a collective investment scheme; and (b) those which allow a society or subsidiary undertaking to use derivative investments in order to limit the extent to which it, or a connected undertaking, will be affected by changes in interest rates, exchange rates, any index of retail prices, any index of residential property prices, any index of the prices of securities, or the creditworthiness of any borrower(s).
llm_rationale: Transaction values below/up to £100,000 determine permitted exemptions.
